query:
Q1 — Option A. from_categories_with_policy(cats, adaptive) as the new constructor,              
  from_categories delegates with the default. No callsite breakage, clean API. The              
  boosted_categories precedent confirms this is the established pattern in this codebase — follow 
  it exactly.                                                     
                                                                                                  
  Q2 — Option B. Summary text shows only adaptive categories; JSON includes all. Operators care
  about what deviates from the default. Printing "decision: pinned, pattern: pinned, convention:
  pinned..." is noise that obscures the signal. "adaptive: [lesson-learned]" is the signal. JSON  
  gets the full picture for tooling. This matches how context_status handles everything else —
  surfaces anomalies and notable states, not a recitation of defaults.                            
                                                                  
  Q3 — Yes, pinned is the correct default for runtime add_category. Domain packs define what
  categories exist for a domain; whether those categories participate in retention is a deployment
   decision, not a domain definition decision. If a domain pack could silently opt its categories 
  into adaptive behavior, a misconfigured pack could enable retention processing the operator
  never intended. Pinned is the safe default. Operators who want adaptive behavior for a domain   
  pack category add it to adaptive_categories in their config — one explicit line, intentional
  opt-in. \
Approved to continue

entries.title: ADR-001 crt-031: CategoryAllowlist lifecycle policy constructor, config model, and StatusService wiring
Category retirement in INITIAL_CATEGORIES requires updates in 4 independent locations
ADR-004 nan-008: Emit tracing::warn! When configured_categories Is Empty in compute_cc_at_k
KnowledgeConfig parallel list fields follow the boosted_categories structural pattern
ADR-005 col-028: confirmed_entries Semantic Contract — Explicit Fetch Only, Shipped Without Consumer
